testcase.name: signToken > produces a valid JWT
signToken > defaults to 1h expiry when none supplied
verifyToken — JWT attacks > rejects a token signed with the wrong secret
verifyToken — JWT attacks > rejects an expired token
verifyToken — JWT attacks > rejects a token with algorithm set to none (none-attack)
verifyToken — JWT attacks > rejects a tampered payload
verifyToken — JWT attacks > returns null for a completely invalid string
guestToken > produces a token with type guest
guestToken > each guest token has a unique id
guestToken > fires onGuestToken callback
authenticateJWT — middleware > calls next() for a valid Bearer token
authenticateJWT — middleware > calls next() for a valid cookie token
authenticateJWT — middleware > returns 401 when no token is present
authenticateJWT — middleware > returns 403 for an expired token
authenticateJWT — middleware > returns 403 for a token signed with wrong secret
authenticateJWT — middleware > returns 403 when token does not match stored session (stolen token)
authenticateJWT — middleware > calls next() when token matches stored session exactly
authenticateJWT — middleware > attaches decoded user to req after successful auth
requireAdmin > rejects requests without a token
requireAdmin > rejects non-admin roles
requireAdmin > rejects tokens without a role
requireAdmin > rejects tokens with wrong secret
requireAdmin > rejects expired tokens
requireAdmin > rejects role casing variations like Admin
requireAdmin > allows admin tokens via Bearer header
requireAdmin > allows admin tokens via cookie
requireAdmin — adminActions > non-admin cannot access listSessions
requireAdmin — adminActions > listSessions returns all sessions without exposing tokens
requireAdmin — adminActions > listSessions returns empty array for user with no sessions
requireAdmin — adminActions > revokeSession removes only the targeted session
requireAdmin — adminActions > revokeSession does not affect other users
requireAdmin — adminActions > revokeAllSessions removes all sessions for a user
requireAdmin — adminActions > revokeAllSessions does not affect other users
OAuth provider linking end to end > Google user can link GitHub and later log in with the same user ID
OAuth provider linking end to end > GitHub user can link Google and later log in with the same user ID
storeRefreshToken > stores token under auth:v1:refresh:<userId>
getRefreshToken > returns the stored value
getRefreshToken > returns null when not found
deleteRefreshToken > deletes the correct key
getset > calls redis.getset and returns the previous value
getset > sets expiry when provided
getset > does not call expire when no expiry is given
blacklistToken > sets blacklist key with correct TTL
isBlacklisted > returns true when key exists
isBlacklisted > returns false when key does not exist
incrementRateLimit > increments the rate limit key
incrementRateLimit > sets expiry only on the first increment
incrementRateLimit > does not set expiry on subsequent increments
whitelist > addToWhitelist sets the correct key
whitelist > removeFromWhitelist deletes the correct key
whitelist > isWhitelisted returns true when key exists
whitelist > isWhitelisted returns false when key does not exist
set > calls redis.set with EX when expiry is provided
set > calls redis.set without EX when no expiry
get > returns value from redis
get > returns null when key is missing
resolveIdentity > when provider is already registered > returns the existing user without touching email lookup
resolveIdentity > when no existing user is found > creates and returns a new user
resolveIdentity > when no existing user is found > does not call linkProvider when creating a new user
resolveIdentity > when user exists by email and auto-link is enabled > links the provider and returns the existing user
resolveIdentity > when user exists by email but auto-link is disabled > returns an IDENTITY_CONFLICT result
resolveIdentity > when user exists by email but auto-link is disabled > does not call linkProvider or createUser
createOAuth > creates both providers when both are configured
createOAuth > creates only Google provider when only Google config is provided
createOAuth > creates only GitHub provider when only GitHub config is provided
createOAuth > returns undefined for both when neither provider is configured
createAuthenik8 > returns all expected properties
createAuthenik8 > uses provided redis client instead of initializing one
createAuthenik8 > initializes redis when none is provided
createAuthenik8 > creates oauth when config.oauth is provided
createAuthenik8 > leaves oauth undefined when config.oauth is not provided
createAuthenik8 > uses provided identityAdapter over the default redis adapter
createAuthenik8 > defaults jwtExpiry to 15m when not provided
issueTokens > returns accessToken and refreshToken
issueTokens > calls signToken and generateRefreshToken with correct payload
issueTokensFromProfile > throws when email is not verified
issueTokensFromProfile > throws when email_verified is an unrecognised value
issueTokensFromProfile > accepts email_verified as the string "true"
issueTokensFromProfile > returns tokens for EXISTING_PROVIDER_LOGIN
issueTokensFromProfile > returns tokens for NEW_USER_CREATION
issueTokensFromProfile > throws the message from LINK_REQUIRED result
issueTokensFromProfile > throws generic error for unexpected result types
issueTokensFromProfile > calls resolveOAuth in login mode with null userId
createGitHubProvider > redirect > stores state in Redis and redirects to GitHub
createGitHubProvider > redirect > includes clientId, redirectUri, scope and state in the redirect URL
createGitHubProvider > redirect > stores userId from req.user in Redis state
createGitHubProvider > redirect > stores mode=link when link mode is passed
createGitHubProvider > redirect > skips redirect when headers are already sent
createGitHubProvider > handleCallback > throws when state is missing from query
createGitHubProvider > handleCallback > throws when state is not found in Redis
createGitHubProvider > handleCallback > throws when code is missing from query
createGitHubProvider > handleCallback > throws when GitHub returns no access token
createGitHubProvider > handleCallback > throws when GitHub user fetch fails
createGitHubProvider > handleCallback > throws when no verified primary email is found
createGitHubProvider > handleCallback > returns profile, mode and userId on success
createGitHubProvider > handleCallback > deletes the state key from Redis after success
createGoogleProvider > redirect > stores state in Redis and redirects to Google
createGoogleProvider > redirect > includes required OAuth params in the redirect URL
createGoogleProvider > redirect > sets mode=link when path contains "link"
createGoogleProvider > redirect > stores userId from req.user in Redis state
createGoogleProvider > redirect > returns 500 when Redis throws
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when state is missing
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when state is not found in Redis
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when code is missing
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when token exchange response is not ok
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when no access_token is returned
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when no id_token is returned
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when ID token payload is null
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when email is absent from payload
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when email is not verified
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > throws when issuer is invalid
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > accepts the alternate accounts.google.com issuer
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > returns correct profile, mode and userId on success
createGoogleProvider > handleCallback > deletes the state key from Redis after success
createIdentityEngine > missing email guard > throws when profile has no email
createIdentityEngine > existing provider login > returns EXISTING_PROVIDER_LOGIN when provider is already registered
createIdentityEngine > existing provider login > does not call createUser or linkProvider for existing provider
createIdentityEngine > link mode > returns INVALID_LINK_REQUEST when userId is missing
createIdentityEngine > link mode > links provider and returns LINK_PROVIDER on success
createIdentityEngine > link mode > falls back to findUserByProvider when findUserByEmail returns null after link
createIdentityEngine > link mode > throws LINK_PROVIDER_user resolution failed when both lookups return null
createIdentityEngine > existing user found by email > returns EXISTING_PROVIDER_LOGIN when auto-link is permitted
createIdentityEngine > existing user found by email > returns LINK_REQUIRED when email is unverified and policy forbids auto-link
createIdentityEngine > existing user found by email > accepts email_verified as the string "true"
createIdentityEngine > existing user found by email > returns LINK_REQUIRED when verified but policy disables auto-link
createIdentityEngine > new user creation > returns NEW_USER_CREATION for a brand new user
createIdentityEngine > new user creation > calls createUser with correct args
createIdentityEngine > new user creation > signs tokens with the new user payload
createIncognito > issues a signed guest token when no bearer token is present
createIncognito > accepts a valid authenticated bearer token
createIncognito > rejects invalid bearer tokens
isAllowed > returns true immediately when whitelist is disabled
isAllowed > returns true when IP is in the Redis set
isAllowed > returns true for localhost ::1
isAllowed > returns true for localhost 127.0.0.1
isAllowed > returns true for IP matching a CIDR entry
isAllowed > returns false for IP not in whitelist or CIDR
isAllowed > returns false when Redis throws
addIP > calls sadd and set with the correct keys
addIP > accepts a custom TTL
removeIP > calls srem and del with the correct keys
listIPs > returns only entries whose TTL key still exists
listIPs > returns empty array when no IPs are stored
whiteListMiddleware > calls next() immediately when whitelist is disabled
whiteListMiddleware > calls next() when IP is allowed
whiteListMiddleware > returns 403 when IP is not allowed
whiteListMiddleware > uses x-forwarded-for when trustProxyHeaders is true
whiteListMiddleware > ignores x-forwarded-for when trustProxyHeaders is false
rateLimiterMiddleware > calls next() when rate limit is not exceeded
rateLimiterMiddleware > returns 429 when rate limit is exceeded
rateLimiterMiddleware > calls next() immediately when rate limiter is disabled
helmetMiddleware > returns a passthrough middleware when helmet is disabled
helmetMiddleware > returns a function when helmet is enabled
TokenBucket > allows request when tokens are available
TokenBucket > denies request when tokens are exhausted
TokenBucket > refills tokens based on elapsed time
TokenBucket > caps refilled tokens at capacity
TokenBucket > uses capacity as default when bucket state is empty
TokenBucket > handles null pipeline result gracefully
TokenBucket > sets expiry on the rate limit key after consuming
initializeRateLimiter > returns a TokenBucket instance
createRatelimiter > calls next() when request is allowed
createRatelimiter > returns 429 when request is denied
createRatelimiter > sets X-RateLimit headers on every response
createRatelimiter > sets Retry-After header when denied
createRatelimiter > returns 503 when token bucket is unavailable
testcase.system-out: ◇ injected env (0) from .env // tip: ⌘ multiple files { path: ['.env.local', '.env'] }

testcase.name: createRatelimiter > uses keyGenerator output as the bucket key
RedisLock > acquire > returns a string value when lock is acquired
RedisLock > acquire > returns null when lock is already held
RedisLock > acquire > calls redis.set with PX, ttl and NX options
RedisLock > acquire > uses default ttl of 5000 when none provided
RedisLock > acquire > returns a unique value on each acquisition
RedisLock > release > calls redis.eval with the lua script, key and value
RedisLock > release > passes the correct number of keys (1) to eval
RedisLock > release > resolves without throwing when Redis returns 0 (lock already gone)
findUserByEmail > returns null when no users exist
findUserByEmail > returns the user when email matches
findUserByEmail > returns null when email does not match
findUserByProvider > returns null when no users exist
findUserByProvider > returns the user when provider and providerId match
findUserByProvider > returns null when provider matches but providerId does not
findUserByProvider > returns null when providerId matches but provider does not
createUser > returns a user with a generated id
createUser > stores the correct email and provider
createUser > generates unique ids for different users
linkProvider > throws if user does not exist
linkProvider > appends the new provider to the user
linkProvider > allows finding user by the newly linked provider
dump > returns empty array when store is empty
dump > returns all created users
reset > clears all users from the store
createRedisIdentityAdapter > findUserByEmail > returns null when user does not exist
createRedisIdentityAdapter > findUserByEmail > returns the user when found
createRedisIdentityAdapter > findUserByProvider > returns null when no matching provider
createRedisIdentityAdapter > findUserByProvider > returns the user when provider is linked
createRedisIdentityAdapter > createUser > throws if email lock cannot be acquired
createRedisIdentityAdapter > createUser > throws if provider lock cannot be acquired (and releases email lock)
createRedisIdentityAdapter > createUser > returns existing user if already registered by email
createRedisIdentityAdapter > createUser > returns existing user if already registered by provider
createRedisIdentityAdapter > createUser > creates a brand new user with Redis multi transaction
createRedisIdentityAdapter > linkProvider > throws if provider lock cannot be acquired
createRedisIdentityAdapter > linkProvider > throws if provider is already linked to another user
createRedisIdentityAdapter > linkProvider > throws if user does not exist
createRedisIdentityAdapter > linkProvider > adds new provider (or ignores duplicate) and updates Redis
validateRedisConfig > throws when neither url nor host is provided
validateRedisConfig > throws when URL does not start with redis:// or rediss://
validateRedisConfig > passes for a valid redis:// URL
validateRedisConfig > passes for a valid rediss:// URL
validateRedisConfig > passes when host is provided
getRedisConfig > returns defaults when no options provided
getRedisConfig > overrides host and port from options
getRedisConfig > uses password from options
getRedisConfig > uses REDIS_PORT env var when no port option is given
setupRedis > resolves with redisClient and redisStore on success
setupRedis > calls ping after ready event fires
setupRedis > registers on("error"), on("ready"), on("reconnecting") listeners
setupRedis > rejects and rethrows when Redis emits an error event
setupRedis > rejects when ping throws after ready
setupRedis > merges custom storeOptions with defaults
setupRedis > passes password to Redis constructor when provided
initializeRedisClient > returns a Redis client
initializeRedisClient > returns the same instance on repeated calls (singleton)
RefreshService > generateRefreshToken > generates token, adds jti, and stores it when tokenStore.set exists
RefreshService > generateRefreshToken > does not call set when tokenStore.set is missing
RefreshService > generateRefreshToken > throws if userId is missing
RefreshService > refresh > throws MissingTokenError when no refreshToken is provided
RefreshService > refresh > throws InvalidTokenError when jwt.verify fails
RefreshService > refresh > throws InvalidTokenError when lock cannot be acquired (concurrent refresh)
RefreshService > refresh > throws InvalidTokenError when stored token does not match
RefreshService > refresh > successfully refreshes token (no rotation)
RefreshService > refresh > rotates refresh token when rotateRefreshTokens = true
RefreshService > refresh > detects concurrent refresh during rotation (getset returns unexpected token)
RefreshService > refresh > releases lock in finally block even when an error occurs
requireAdmin > rejects requests without a token
requireAdmin > rejects non-admin roles
requireAdmin > rejects tokens without an admin role
requireAdmin > allows admin tokens
SecurityModule whitelist middleware > does not trust x-forwarded-for by default
SecurityModule whitelist middleware > can trust x-forwarded-for when explicitly enabled
Store > register > throws when a user with that email already exists
Store > register > creates the user when email is not taken
Store > register > calls findByEmail with the provided email
In-memory OAuth User Store > findUserByEmail > returns undefined when user does not exist
In-memory OAuth User Store > findUserByEmail > returns the user when email matches
In-memory OAuth User Store > findUserByProvider > returns undefined when no matching provider
In-memory OAuth User Store > findUserByProvider > finds user by provider + providerId
In-memory OAuth User Store > createUser > creates a new user with UUID, email and initial provider
In-memory OAuth User Store > createUser > adds the user to the store
In-memory OAuth User Store > linkProvider > throws when userId does not exist
In-memory OAuth User Store > linkProvider > adds a new provider if not already linked
In-memory OAuth User Store > linkProvider > does not duplicate an already-linked provider
